title: `Array(Tuple)` with tuple element `size0` leads to logical error and crash
description:
### Company or project name
ClickHouse
### Describe what's wrong
After reading the column size, the cache entry name clashes with the column name
[Here](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/238e70c1902d78bffc0c2e37c4e30465d536fc0d/src/DataTypes/Serializations/ISerialization.cpp#L152)
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Nested(size0 String, c1 Int)) ENGINE = Log();
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0.size0, c0.c1) VALUES (['A'], [1]);
SELECT c0.size0 FROM t0; -- Logical error
SELECT 1 FROM t0 GROUP BY c0.size0, c0.c1; -- Crash
```
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/448643ca-e5d4-41b8-9d0b-57132b75393f
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (size0 TEXT, c1 INTEGER, c2 REAL);
INSERT INTO t1 (size0, c1, c2) VALUES ('B', 2, 1.5), (NULL, 3, 2.5);
SELECT size0, c1 FROM t1 WHERE c2 > 2.0;
CREATE TABLE t2 AS SELECT size0, COUNT(*) AS count FROM t1 GROUP BY size0;
key_bug_features: Cache entry name clashes with column name
